author: Saunak Karnati
description: Record audio from the ribbon, save it to your vault, and create Groq-powered transcription notes. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

name: Flux TTS

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Add opt-in automatic transcripts for pasted YouTube and public media links, rendered as citation-style footnotes. This release keeps AI cleanup and timestamp controls, fixes actionable Obsidian scan warnings, and retains Obsidian 1.12 compatibility.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Support Obsidian 1.12 by backporting the settings screen to the stable imperative API. This release restores updates for stable-channel Obsidian users and synchronizes the plugin metadata and bundled assets.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fix attestation permissions

The attest-build-provenance@v2 action requires attestations: write permission in addition to id-token: write.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Add live waveform + inline transcription, fix mobile ribbon label, release 0.3.0

- Live recording waveform (status bar on desktop, floating pill on mobile),
  reading the existing analyser node exposed via RecorderController.getAnalyser().
- Inline transcription at the cursor: new toggle/start/stop commands insert the
  transcript where the cursor is and append a footnote linking back to the
  recording ([^id] renders as a superscript reference).
- Mobile ribbon menu now reflects recording state: update the internal ribbon
  item title (mobile rebuilds its menu from it) and swap the mic/stop icon.
- Add public ROADMAP.md; condense README; document the Obsidian 1.13.0
  requirement (settings tab uses the 1.13.0 getSettingDefinitions API).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Migrates the settings tab off the deprecated display() override onto Obsidian's declarative getSettingDefinitions() API (bumps minAppVersion to 1.13.0). Also fixes several correctness and release-hygiene issues: drops versions.json from release assets (Obsidian reads it from the repo, not release assets), narrows error handling from `any` to `unknown` with proper message extraction, replaces a `document` reference with `activeDocument` so the mic-disconnect notice works correctly in popout windows, and removes unnecessary type assertions and an unused import.

No user-facing behavior change; settings UI and functionality are unchanged from 0.2.0.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Migrates the plugin from a bare JavaScript prototype to TypeScript, split into focused modules (recording, transcription, template rendering, settings) and bundled to main.js with esbuild. Fixes a filename-templating bug where an unbalanced placeholder (e.g. a stray `{{time}`) silently produced a malformed filename instead of being rejected. Adds store-readiness features required for Obsidian community plugin submission: masked API key storage, a folder-picker for note/audio save locations, a Markdown-file-based note template, graceful handling of mic disconnects and low-signal recordings, and a GitHub Actions release pipeline.

No breaking changes for existing users; settings from 0.1.0 migrate automatically.
